quests.lesson: Every job starts with square stock.
zachIntro: Welcome to the bay. It's empty, it's ours, and it's America's.
First job's on the board. Cut some stock.
zachOutro: Square stock, square parts.
Now let's make some chips.

quests.lesson: Routing work across stations is the job.
zachIntro: Mill it, deburr it, ship it.
The route is the recipe.
zachOutro: First part out the door. Feel that?
That's a shop being born.

quests.lesson: Setups, not cycle time, eat small shops alive.
zachIntro: Big iron just landed.
Your first setup decides your whole day.
zachOutro: Clean setup, clean parts.
Now we can quote real work.

quests.lesson: Machine monitoring turns invisible downtime into money.
zachIntro: That machine's been lying to you about its uptime.
Let's wire it up and see the truth.
zachOutro: See that idle bar? That's money leaking.
Now we can fix what we can see.

quests.lesson: Offline tool presetting reclaims spindle time.
zachIntro: Touching off on the machine is paying spindle rates for measurement.
Preset offline. Cut sooner.
zachOutro: Ten tools preset and that setup flew.
This is how the best toolrooms in the country run.

quests.lesson: Shift handoffs are where shops silently bleed.
zachIntro: Night shift can't read your mind.
Seven clean handoffs. In a row. Go.
zachOutro: Seven for seven. No mystery scrap, no lost notes.
THIS is what separates good shops from great ones.

quests.lesson: Automation ROI = downtime now for throughput forever.
zachIntro: The arm's in the crate. Installing it will hurt for a shift.
Robots don't fix a bad process — they run it faster. Ours is good. Let's go.
zachOutro: Twenty cycles untouched by human hands.
Tonight, we try lights-out.

quests.lesson: Your real shop deserves real visibility.
zachIntro: Enough simulation. You've got real iron on a real floor.
Connect one machine. For real. I'll wait.
zachOutro: That gold LIVE badge? That's a real machine, talking.
Welcome to the connected shop.

zachIntro: Your crew. Your floor. Seven real clean handoffs this week.
zachOutro: Your night shift just thanked you. Quietly. In their heads.

quests.lesson: This is your shop and your machinist.
zachIntro: Before we cut anything — who's running this shop?
Pick your machinist. Any of them can build an empire.
zachOutro: Looking sharp. Let's go make chips.

quests.lesson: No material, no parts. Know your distributor.
zachIntro: Can't cut air. Call NOX Metals — Dana will set you up.
Start with 6061. It's forgiving and it's everywhere.
zachOutro: Bar stock on the dock. Smell that? That's inventory.
Watch lead times on steel — plan ahead or pay rush.

quests.lesson: G-code parameters come from math and material, not guessing.
zachIntro: The VMC won't run until the program's right. Fill in the blanks.
RPM comes off the material cert. Offsets tell it where the part lives.
zachOutro: You just wrote real G-code. That runs on real machines.
Every parameter had a reason. Remember the reasons.

quests.lesson: Aerospace = traceability. No cert, no part.
zachIntro: Meridian wants certs on everything. Order accordingly.
Ten percent more from NOX beats a hundred percent rejection.
zachOutro: Certed material, clean paper trail, shipped on time.
THAT is aerospace manufacturing.

quests.lesson: The chalkboard corner is where the floor's math lives.
zachIntro: See that chalkboard in the corner? That's Shop Class.
Every lesson unlocks something real on this floor.
zachOutro: First lesson down. Five more on the board.
Come back anytime — the chalk doesn't wear out.
